description: The world's second-largest continent, home to 54 countries and an incredible diversity of cultures, languages, and landscapes.
fact: Africa contains more countries than any other continent.

description: Spanning from the Arctic to the tip of South America, the Americas cover the entire western hemisphere.
fact: The Amazon rainforest in South America produces 20% of the world's oxygen.

description: The southernmost continent, almost entirely covered by ice, with no permanent human population.
fact: Antarctica holds about 70% of the world's fresh water in its ice sheets.

description: The largest and most populous continent on Earth, stretching from the Middle East to the Pacific Ocean.
fact: Asia is home to over 4.7 billion people, more than half the world's population.

description: A peninsula of Eurasia, Europe is known for its rich history, diverse cultures, and political unity through the European Union.
fact: Europe has the highest density of UNESCO World Heritage Sites of any continent.

description: A region of the Pacific Ocean encompassing Australia, Melanesia, Micronesia, and Polynesia.
fact: Oceania contains over 10,000 islands spread across the Pacific Ocean.
